context.projectOverview: agent-bober telegram adapter. Sprints 1-2 DONE: src/telegram/{whitelist,outbound,bot,router}.ts + handlers/capture.ts. sendSafe is the sole outbound funnel; bot.ts has a command-dispatch stub (router.classify → /start=helpReply, other commands='Unknown command', plain text=capture). Sprint 3 fills the command dispatch with /today, /priority, /decide X vs Y → priority hub query → numbered ranked reply.

priority-hub query surface (curator pins exact import export or execa CLI)

contract.title: Scoped prioritization commands (/today, /priority, /decide X vs Y)
contract.description: Handle /today, /priority, and /decide X vs Y by parsing an ephemeral per-question scope from the command text, invoking the priority hub (spec-20260628-priority-hub) with that scope, and replying with a numbered ranked summary through the sendSafe funnel. /decide X vs Y produces a decision-mode scope filtered to the two options.

successCriteria.description: `npm run build` (tsc) compiles the command parser and prioritize handler with zero TypeScript errors.
A unit test shows the parser extracts exactly the two options from "/decide X vs Y" into a decision-mode scope, and maps /today to a time-horizon scope and /priority to a general scope.

A unit test shows the prioritize handler calls the hub query with the parsed scope and renders the returned findings as a numbered list in the same order the hub returned (using an injected fake hub returning fixture findings).
A unit test confirms the rendered reply contains only finding titles/summaries and is produced through the sendSafe funnel (no raw domain detail beyond titles/summaries).
Sending /priority to the running bot returns a numbered ranked list sourced from the agent-bober hub pool.

nonGoals: Do not implement ranking, relevance filtering, or lens-panel judging — invoke the hub query from spec-20260628-priority-hub.
Do not persist or store the per-question scope — it is ephemeral, derived from the command text only.
Do not include raw domain detail beyond titles/summaries in the reply; route all output through sendSafe.
Do not add task-mutation commands (done/snooze/drop) in this sprint.
Do not let the LLM be invoked from this adapter — the hub owns any model calls.
stopConditions: The command-parser unit tests for /today, /priority, and /decide pass and `npm run build` exits zero.
Invoking the prioritize handler with a fixture hub returns a numbered list whose order matches the hub's returned ranking.
/decide A vs B yields a scope containing exactly options A and B.
contract.definitionOfDone: Sending /today, /priority, or /decide X vs Y causes the bot to call the priority hub with an ephemeral scope parsed from the command (decision mode filtered to X and Y; today and priority use a time-horizon or general scope) and reply with a numbered, ranked summary of findings. The scope is never stored; each command re-derives it from its own text, and the reply carries only titles and short summaries.
assumptions: The hub exposes a scoped prioritization query (decision / general / filtered modes), per research §3a lines 115-119; the adapter invokes it per Q1's hybrid rule (import if exported, else execa).
/decide options are split on the literal " vs " separator from the command text.
Findings carry a title/summary suitable for control-plane display (Finding schema, research §3a lines 122-126).
outOfScope: Defining the Finding ranking algorithm or the lens-panel passes (owned by spec-20260628-priority-hub).
Time-zone handling for /today beyond passing a time-horizon scope to the hub.
contract.generatorNotes: Add src/telegram/handlers/prioritize.ts and extend the command dispatch from Sprint 2's router. Parser: /today -> {mode:'general', horizon:'today'}; /priority -> {mode:'general'}; /decide <rest> -> split rest on ' vs ' (case-insensitive, trim) -> {mode:'decision', options:[X,Y]}. Handler accepts an injected hub-query function (default imports the hub query export if present, else execa `agent-bober` hub command) returning ranked findings; render as a numbered list of `title` (+ optional one-line summary). All output via sendSafe. Keep titles/summaries only — do not expand evidence or domain payloads. ESM .js imports, `import type`, no `any`.
contract.evaluatorNotes: Verify /decide parsing yields exactly two trimmed options. Verify the rendered order equals the fixture hub's returned order (the adapter does not re-rank). Verify scope is not written to disk anywhere. Verify reply goes through sendSafe and contains only titles/summaries. Run build + the parser/handler unit tests.

principles:
# Project Principles

> These principles guide all planning, generation, and evaluation in the Bober pipeline.
> Updated: 2026-04-16

## Mission

Agent-bober is a multi-agent harness that builds applications autonomously using any LLM. It orchestrates a pipeline of specialized agents (Researcher, Planner, Curator, Generator, Evaluator) and supports Claude, GPT, Gemini, and Ollama. It ships as an npm CLI + MCP server for Claude Code, Cursor, and Windsurf.

## Users

- **Primary:** Developers using Claude Code, Cursor, or Windsurf who want autonomous feature generation with quality gates
- **Secondary:** Teams integrating agent-bober programmatically via the TypeScript API or MCP server
- Technical level: intermediate to advanced — users understand LLMs, CI/CD, and modern JS tooling

## Quality Standards

- **Type safety:** TypeScript strict mode with all strict flags (`noUnusedLocals`, `noUnusedParameters`, `noImplicitReturns`, `noFallthroughCasesInSwitch`, `isolatedModules`). Zero type errors is a hard gate.
- **Lint compliance:** ESLint flat config with `consistent-type-imports` enforced, `no-explicit-any` warned, unused vars errored (with `_` prefix escape). Zero lint errors is a hard gate.
- **Test coverage:** Vitest for unit tests. Tests are collocated with source (`*.test.ts` next to `*.ts`). Tests run against the real project when practical (e.g., scanner tests scan agent-bober itself).
- **Build integrity:** `tsc` must produce clean output. Build is a required evaluator strategy.

## Technical Principles

### Follow

- **ESM everywhere.** `"type": "module"` in package.json. All imports use `.js` extensions for NodeNext resolution. No CommonJS.
- **Provider-agnostic interfaces.** All LLM interaction goes through `providers/types.ts` — provider-specific SDKs are wrapped by adapters (`anthropic.ts`, `openai.ts`, `google.ts`). Never leak SDK types outside adapter files.
- **Zod for config validation.** All configuration schemas are Zod schemas in `config/schema.ts`. Runtime config loading uses `z.parse()`. No hand-rolled validation.
- **Contract-based architecture.** PlanSpecs, SprintContracts, and EvalResults are the communication protocol between agents. They live in `contracts/` and are JSON files on disk in `.bober/`.
- **Filesystem state.** All mutable state (specs, contracts, handoffs, eval results, history) is stored as JSON files in `.bober/`. No database, no in-memory global state.
- **Section comments.** Use unicode box-drawing section headers: `// -- Section Name ------` to organize long files.
- **Small utility modules.** Utils in `utils/` are focused single-purpose files (`fs.ts`, `git.ts`, `logger.ts`). Keep them small.
- **Conventional commits.** Format: `type: description` where type is `feat`, `fix`, `docs`, `release`, `refactor`, `test`, `chore`. Sprint-generated commits use `bober(sprint-N): description`.
- **Use `type` imports.** ESLint enforces `consistent-type-imports` -- import types with `import type { ... }` syntax.
- **Prefix unused params with `_`.** The `_` prefix is the only escape hatch for unused variables/parameters.

### Avoid

- **No `any` without justification.** `no-explicit-any` is a warning; aim for zero. Use `unknown` + type narrowing instead.
- **No SDK lock-in.** Never import `@anthropic-ai/sdk` or `openai` outside of their respective adapter files in `providers/`.
- **No synchronous filesystem ops.** All fs operations use `node:fs/promises`. No `fs.readFileSync` etc.
- **No barrel re-exports for deep internals.** `src/index.ts` exports the public API only. Internal modules import directly.
- **No test mocks for filesystem.** Tests that need filesystem state create temp directories and clean up. The scanner tests run against the real codebase.

## Design Principles

N/A -- no user interface. Agent-bober is a CLI tool and programmatic library. The MCP server exposes tools, not UI.
